rationale: The only resume change was employer/company names (re:cinq, Optilyz, GISCollective → Acme Software, Pinepoint Systems, Northwind Labs), which carry no job-relevant signal here. Both evaluations land on the identical score (6/10) and the identical core conclusion: strong full-stack/multi-language engineering but no explicit experimentation-platform, feature-flagging, telemetry, or AI-driven growth experience. The wording differences (emphasizing TypeScript/Node.js vs. C/C++/D, dropping the Dunnhumby mention) are ordinary run-to-run rewording rather than reasoning keyed off the company names — note GISCollective is still credited as a strength in both versions. No concern or score shift attaches to the changed employer names.
